Acta v0.2.0-pilot record · Generated from accepted canonical state (learning/tag-filter-understanding.md) after explicit acceptance (chat 2026-07-23 — gaps export pasted and confirmed after completing all six questions) · Never generated from browser state.

quiz record · ACCEPTED

Understanding check: tag-filter change — 5/6, one named gap

Six evidence-grounded questions against the merged tag-filter change: five answered correctly; the unknown-tag fallback was missed and is recorded as a named gap with its evidence locations. Diagnostic only — never a merge gate.

Result

5 / 6score
1named gaps
diagnosticnever a merge gate

Six evidence-grounded questions against the merged tag-filter change: five answered correctly; the unknown-tag fallback was missed and is recorded as a named gap with its evidence locations. Diagnostic only — never a merge gate.

Reflection

I had the no-JS story right but assumed unknown tags would just blank the list — the empty state decision had not landed in my head.

Answers

#QuestionAnswered
01 Where does filterByTag run? In Node tests and the browser — same import ✓
02 With JavaScript disabled, what does ?tag=git show? The full catalog — chips are plain links, filtering needs JS ✓
03 What renders for ?tag=deploy (a tag no skill has)? A blank list
correct: The accepted explain-and-suggest empty state		 ✗
04 What state does the page persist between visits? Nothing — the URL is the only state ✓
05 Why does popstate reuse renderFromUrl instead of its own handler? So back/forward can never diverge from a fresh load ✓
06 What was rejected at STOP-01 during the build? Fetching tag suggestions from a remote API ✓

Gaps to close

unknown-tag fallback

What renders for ?tag=deploy (a tag no skill has)?

STOP-01 kept unknown tags empty and interface-directions chose direction B: the empty state names the miss and offers the real tag set.

Sources

  1. [E1] src/catalog.mjs — filterByTag
  2. [E2] src/url-tag.mjs — parseTagFromSearch
  3. [E3] src/app.mjs — browser glue
  4. [E4] build-with-notes session record — STOP-01 resolution

Acta is a print-first engineering record. Fact = directly supported; Inference = reasoned from evidence; Simplification = deliberately reduced model. This record renders accepted canonical state only; open decisions live in the companion instrument.
